|colspan=9 align="left"|Santos has the national record of 17 consecutive unbeaten games.
|-
|}

==Players==
{{For|all past and present players who have appeared for the national team|List of Greece international footballers}}

===Current squad===
The following players were called for the [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly matches]] against [[Sweden men's national football team|Sweden]] and [[Italy national football team|Italy]] on 4 and 7 June 2026, respectively.
<br>''Caps and goals as of 7 June 2026, after the match against [[Italy national football team|Italy]].''<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/match/2034445/ | title=Kosovo-Greece &#124; UEFA Nations League 2023 &#124; UEFA.com |website=[[UEFA]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=175&data=9|title=Most Greece Caps - EU-Football.info |website=eu-football.info}}</ref>

{{nat fs g start}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the Greece squad within the last twelve months.
<!--Sorted by position, most recent call-up, caps, goals, and last name.--> <!--Only update at the end of the game.-->

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Konstantinos Karetsas|Kostas Karetsas]]|age={{Birth date and age|2007|11|19|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=3|club=[[K.R.C. Genk|Genk]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|PAR}}, 27 March 2026}}<sup>INJ</sup>

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Manolis Siopis]]|age={{Birth date and age|1994|5|14|df=y}}|caps=42|goals=1|club=[[Panathinaikos F.C.|Panathinaikos]]|clubnat=GRE|latest=v. {{fb|BLR}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Dimitrios Pelkas|Dimitris Pelkas]]|age={{Birth date and age|1993|10|26|df=y}}|caps=47|goals=5|club=[[PAOK FC|PAOK]]|clubnat=GRE|latest=v. {{fb|DEN}}, 8 September 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Fotis Ioannidis]]|age={{Birth date and age|2000|1|10|df=y}}|caps=21|goals=6|club=[[Sporting CP]]|clubnat=POR|latest=v. {{fb|DEN}}, 12 October 2025 <sup>INJ</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Pavlos Pantelidis]]|age={{Birth date and age|2002|09|16|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Panathinaikos F.C.|Panathinaikos]]|clubnat=GRE|latest=v. {{fb|SCO}}, 9 October 2025 <sup>PRE</sup>}}
{{nat fs break}}
; Notes
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Unavailable due to injury
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad/standby
*<sup>RET</sup> = Retired from international football
*<sup>SUS</sup> = Suspended from the matches in question
{{nat fs end}}

:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Greece.''

|-
!Name
!Period
!Notes

|-

|-

|-

|-
|[[Kostas Polychroniou]]||1961–1967||
|-

|-
|[[Mimis Domazos]]||1970–1979||
|-

|-
|[[Anthimos Kapsis]]||1982{{0}}{{0}}{{0}}{{0}}{{0}}||
|-
|[[Nikos Anastopoulos]]||1983–1988||
|-

|-

|-
|[[Nikos Machlas]]||1999||

|[[Angelos Basinas]]||2007–2009||{{small|European Championship captain ([[UEFA Euro 2008|2008]])}}
|-
|[[Giorgos Karagounis]]||2009–2014||{{small|World Cup captain ([[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]])}}<br>{{small|European Championship captain ([[UEFA Euro 2012|2012]])}}<br>{{small|World Cup captain ([[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]])}}
|-
|[[Dimitris Salpingidis]]||2014||
|-
|[[Vasilis Torosidis]]||2014–2019||
|-
|[[Kostas Stafylidis]]||2019–2020||
|-
|[[Anastasios Bakasetas]]||2020–||
|}

==Competitive record==
===Competitive results===
These are Greece's results in the major competition that they have participated in. The results in the main tournament have been listed directly in the total column.

|-

!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;" colspan=2|Greece's European Championship history

!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|First match
|{{fb|NED}} 1–0 {{fb-rt|GRE}}<br>([[Naples]], Italy; 11 June 1980)
|-
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|Biggest win
|{{fb|POR}} 1–2 {{fb-rt|GRE}}<br>([[Porto]], Portugal; 12 June 2004)<br>{{fb|FRA|1974}} 0–1 {{fb-rt|GRE}}<br>([[Lisbon]], Portugal; 25 June 2004)<br>{{fb|GRE}} 1–0 ([[Overtime (sports)#Association football|a.e.t.]]) {{fb-rt|CZE}}<br>([[Porto]], Portugal; 1 July 2004)<br>{{fb|POR}} [[UEFA Euro 2004 Final|0–1]] {{fb-rt|GRE}}<br>([[Lisbon]], Portugal; 4 July 2004)<br>{{fb|GRE}} 1–0 {{fb-rt|RUS}}<br>([[Warsaw]], Poland; 16 June 2012)
|-
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|Biggest defeat
|{{fb|GRE}} 1–3 {{fb-rt|TCH}}<br>([[Rome]], Italy; 14 June 1980)<br>{{fb|GRE}} 0–2 {{fb-rt|SWE}}<br>([[Salzburg]], Austria; 10 June 2008)<br>{{fb|GER}} 4–2 {{fb-rt|GRE}}<br>([[Gdańsk]], Poland; 22 June 2012)
|-
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|Best result
|'''Champions''' ([[UEFA Euro 2004 Final|2004]])
|-
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|Worst result
|Group stage ([[UEFA Euro 1980|1980]], [[UEFA Euro 2008|2008]])
|}

!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|0
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|4
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|—
|}
:''*Draws include knockout matches decided on [[Penalty shoot-out (association football)|penalty kicks]].''

{| class="wikitable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"
|-
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;" colspan=2|Greece's Confederations Cup history
|-
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|First match
|{{fb|BRA}} 3–0 {{fb-rt|GRE}}<br>([[Leipzig]], Germany; 16 June 2005)
|-
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|Biggest win
|None
|-
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|Biggest defeat
| {{fb|BRA}} 3–0 {{fb-rt|GRE}}<br>([[Leipzig]], Germany; 16 June 2005)
|-
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|Best result
|Group stage ([[2005 FIFA Confederations Cup|2005]])
|-
!style="color:white; background:#0066bc;"|Worst result
|Group stage ([[2005 FIFA Confederations Cup|2005]])
|}

|-

!Result
!Position

!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}

|{{flagicon|Greece|old}} [[1896 Summer Olympics|1896]]||colspan=9|''No football tournament was held''
|-

|-

|-

|-
|{{flagicon|Australia}} [[Association football at the 1956 Summer Olympics|1956]]||colspan=9|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[Football at the 1960 Summer Olympics|1960]]||colspan=9 rowspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Japan|1947}} [[Football at the 1964 Summer Olympics|1964]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[Football at the 1968 Summer Olympics|1968]]
|-
|{{flagicon|West Germany}} [[Football at the 1972 Summer Olympics|1972]]
|-

|-

|-

|-

==FIFA ranking history==
Greece's history in the [[FIFA World Ranking]]. The table shows the position that Greece held in December of each year (and the current position as of 2024), as well as the highest and lowest positions annually.

{| class="wikitable sortable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align: center; font-size: 100%;"

!width=105|Position

!width=80|Lowest

!Total||665||253||159||254||835||908
|-
|}
The game against Great Britain's Olympic Team (1952) was recognized as an official game of the Greece National Team by the Hellenic Football Federation.

==Honours==
[[File:Griechische Nationalmannschaft bei der Siegerehrung.jpg|thumb|The Greece national team at the [[UEFA Euro 2004]] trophy ceremony]]

===Continental===
* '''[[UEFA European Championship]]'''<ref>{{cite web |title=Most titles |url=https://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/history/winners |website=uefa.com |access-date=18 August 2025 |location=Online}}</ref>
** {{gold1}} '''Champions:''' [[UEFA Euro 2004|2004]]

===Regional===
* '''[[Football at the Mediterranean Games|Mediterranean Games]]'''<ref>{{cite web |last1=Di Maggio |first1=Roberto |last2=Stokkermans |first2=Karel |last3=Jönsson |first3=Mikael |title=Mediterranean Cup and Mediterranean Games - Overview |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/medgames.html |website=rsssf.org |access-date=18 August 2025 |location=Online |date=11 April 2024}}</ref>
** {{gold1}} '''Gold medal:''' [[Football at the 1951 Mediterranean Games|1951]], [[Football at the 1991 Mediterranean Games|1991]]
** {{bronze3}} Bronze medal: [[Football at the 1997 Mediterranean Games|1997]]
* '''[[Mediterranean Cup (men's football)|Mediterranean Cup]]'''<ref>{{cite web |last1=Jönsson |first1=Mikael |last2=Testero |first2=Giampaolo |title=East Mediterranean Cup 1950-1953 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/medcup53.html |website=rsssf.org |access-date=18 August 2025 |location=Online |date=11 April 2024}}</ref>
** {{silver2}} Runners-up: [[1950–53 Mediterranean Cup|1950–53]]
* '''[[Balkan Cup]]'''<ref>{{cite web |last1=De Dekker |first1=Guy |last2=Stokkermans |first2=Karel |title=Balkan Cup (for Nations) |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesb/balkancup.html |website=rsssf.org |access-date=18 August 2025 |location=Online |date=26 November 2020}}</ref>
** {{silver2}} Runners-up: [[1934–35 Balkan Cup|1934–35]]
** {{bronze3}} Third place: [[1929–31 Balkan Cup|1929–31]], [[1935 Balkan Cup|1935]], [[1936 Balkan Cup|1936]]

===Friendly===
* '''[[Cyprus International Football Tournament]]'''<ref>{{cite web |title=Cyprus International Tournament 1999 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cypr-intltourn99.html |website=rsssf.org |access-date=18 August 2025 |location=Online |date=27 February 2001 |quote=Greece 1-0 Belgium}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=King |first1=Ian |title=Cyprus International Tournament 2006 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cypr-intltourn06.html |website=rsssf.org |access-date=18 August 2025 |location=Online |date=7 February 2007 |quote=Greece 2-0 Kazakhstan}}</ref>
** {{gold1}} '''Champions:''' 1999, 2006
* '''[[Sir Stanley Matthews Cup]]'''
** {{silver2}} '''Runners-up:''' 1988<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.canadasoccer.com/this-day-in-football-from-28-may-to-3-june|title=This Day in Football from 28 May to 3 June|date=28 May 2012|access-date=24 June 2026|website=Soccer Canada}}</ref>

===Awards===
* '''[[Laureus World Sports Award for Team of the Year|Laureus World Team of the Year]]''': [[Laureus World Sports Award for Team of the Year|2005]]
* '''[[World Soccer (magazine)#Men's World Team of the Year|World Soccer Team of the Year]]''': [[World Soccer (magazine)|2004]]
* '''[[FIFA Confederations Cup records and statistics#Awards|FIFA Confederations Cup Fair Play Award]]''': [[2005 FIFA Confederations Cup|2005]]

===Summary===
{| class="wikitable" style="width:90%; font-size:90%; text-align:center;"
|-
!Competition!!{{gold1}}!!{{silver2}}!!{{bronze3}}!!Total
|-
|align=left|[[UEFA European Championship]]
|1||0||0||1
|-
!Total!!1!!0!!0!!1
|}

* [[Greece national under-19 football team]]

==Notes==
{{notelist}}

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==Further reading==
* Αρβανίτης, Στάθης (2004). ''Εθνική Ελλάδος Ποδοσφαίρου 1929–2004'' {{in lang|el}}. Εκδόσεις Καστανιώτης. {{ISBN|960-03-3778-0}}.
* Μαμουζέλος, Γιάννης Ν. & Νταβέλος, Θοδωρής (2007). ''100 χρόνια Εθνική Ελλάδας'' {{in lang|el}}. Αθήνα, Ελλάδα: Εκδόσεις Άγκυρα. {{ISBN|978-960-42-2497-5}}.
* Φασούλας, Χρήστος & Κοντολέων, Δομήνικος (2008). ''Εθνική Ελλάδος γεια σου!'' {{in lang|el}}. Εκδόσεις Παπαδόπουλος. {{ISBN|978-960-41-2819-8}}.
* Σαμπράκος, Βασίλης (2018). ''Εξηγώντας το Θαύμα'' {{in lang|el}}. Εκδόσεις Τόπος. {{ISBN|978-960-499-273-7}}.

==External links==
{{Commons category}}
* {{official website|http://www.epo.gr/}} {{in lang|el}}
